title: This $4 Million Rental Stock Buy Signals Confidence in Data Center Demand
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Anchor Capital Management increased its stake in McGrath RentCorp by 40,352 shares ($4.49 million) in Q1 2026, signaling confidence in data center demand. McGrath's rental operations revenue climbed 5%, with TRS-RenTelco rental revenue jumping 13% due to accelerating data center buildouts. The company maintains a 35-year dividend increase streak and offers exposure to infrastructure spending with recurring revenue.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Anchor Capital's increased investment signals confidence in the company's growth prospects, particularly in data center demand. Strong Q1 results show 5% rental revenue growth and 13% growth in TRS-RenTelco segment. The company's 35-year dividend increase streak and stable adjusted EBITDA demonstrate financial strength and shareholder-friendly policies, despite broader market underperformance.
Mentioned as Anchor Capital's largest holding (22.8% of AUM) but no specific news or analysis provided about the company in the article.
Listed as one of Anchor Capital's top holdings (18.3% of AUM) but no specific news or analysis provided about the company in the article.

title: What to Know About This Fund’s $14 Million Patrick Industries Exit After a Tough Quarter
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Anchor Capital Management fully exited its position in Patrick Industries during Q1 2026, selling 116,967 shares worth approximately $14.46 million. The exit reduced Patrick Industries from 11.3% of the fund's assets to zero. Patrick Industries faces headwinds from softening RV and housing demand, though the company showed strength in marine and powersports segments with revenue of $997 million and maintained solid free cash flow of $194 million.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The fund's complete exit of an 11.3% position signals loss of confidence. Stock has plunged nearly 40% since February and underperformed S&P 500 by 17 percentage points. While the company maintains operational strength and free cash flow, macro pressures in RV and housing markets are weighing on investor sentiment.
Mentioned as a top holding in Anchor Capital's portfolio ($21.10 million, 22.8% of AUM) with no specific performance commentary provided in the article.
Listed as a top holding in Anchor Capital's portfolio ($20.43 million, 22.0% of AUM) with no specific performance commentary provided in the article.

title: Hillman Acquires Delaney Hardware; Expands Pro Distribution Categories Adding Door Hardware
author: Not Specified
description: Hillman Solutions Corp. has acquired Delaney Hardware, a U.S.-based supplier of door hardware and builder's hardware for residential, multifamily, and commercial construction. The acquisition expands Hillman's presence in the pro distribution channel and is expected to generate over $10 million in net sales during fiscal 2026. This marks Hillman's second acquisition in 2026, following the recent acquisition of Campbell Chain & Fittings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The acquisition expands Hillman's strategic presence in the pro distribution channel, adds new product categories (door hardware), and is expected to contribute over $10 million in net sales in fiscal 2026. This represents execution of the company's long-term growth strategy through acquisitions, which is viewed favorably by the market.

title: Hillman Acquires Campbell Chain & Fittings; Expands Industrial MRO Presence
author: Na
description: Hillman Solutions Corp. (NASDAQ: HLMN) announced the acquisition of Campbell Chain & Fittings from Apex Tool Group, a premier manufacturer of industrial chain and chain-related products founded in 1919. The acquisition expands Hillman's industrial MRO sector presence and complements its 2024 Koch Industries acquisition, adding grade 70, 80, and 100 chain products to its portfolio. Hillman expects over $20 million in net sales from Campbell during fiscal year 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The acquisition strengthens Hillman's position in the industrial MRO sector, a key growth area. It adds established manufacturing capabilities, expands product offerings with high-grade chain products, and is expected to contribute over $20 million in net sales in 2026. Management views this as part of their strategic growth strategy.

title: Hillman (HLMN) Q2 Revenue Jumps 6%
author: Jesterai
description: Hillman Solutions reported Q2 2025 financial results with revenue of $402.8 million, exceeding analyst expectations. The company showed sales growth of 6.2% and adjusted EPS of $0.17, while managing supply chain challenges and tariff impacts.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed performance with positive revenue growth and earnings, but challenges in free cash flow and ongoing supply chain complexities related to tariffs
